paragraphs.source: Prices of memory are going crazy. SemiAnalysis has been calling this out for over a year since late 2024. The scariest thing is that we aren't even close to the peak. We go through fab by fab production and expansion versus detailed end market demand by memory type to forecast memory revenue, pricing, and margin better than anyone else. This has all been detailed in the SemiAnalysis memory model for a while, but we will share it more publicly today. First some background.

inline_elements.text: forecast memory revenue, pricing, and margin

paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Prices of memory are going crazy. SemiAnalysis has been calling this out for over a year since late 2024. The scariest thing is that we aren't even close to the peak. We go through [fab by fab production](https://semianalysis.com/memory-model/) and [expansion](https://semianalysis.com/memory-model/) versus [detailed end market demand](https://semianalysis.com/memory-model/) by memory type to [forecast memory revenue, pricing, and margin](https://semianalysis.com/memory-model/) better than anyone else. This has all been detailed in the [SemiAnalysis memory model](https://semianalysis.com/memory-model/) for a while, but we will share it more publicly today. First some background.

pro.text: 存储器价格正陷入狂热。自 2024 年底以来，SemiAnalysis 已经连续一年多发出预警。最令人胆寒的是，我们距离顶点还差得很远。我们将逐个晶圆厂（fab）的产量与扩产计划，同各存储器类型的终端市场需求逐一对比，以此预测存储器营收、定价与利润率，精准度无人能及。这些内容早已在SemiAnalysis 存储模型中详尽披露，今天我们将向公众公开更多细节。先来介绍一些背景信息。

sections.title: The Inevitability of Memory Cycles: A History of Booms and Busts
sections.title_translation: 存储周期的必然性：繁荣与萧条的历史

paragraphs.source: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Since its commercial introduction in the 1970s, DRAM has benefited from the two scaling laws that defined the semiconductor industry: Moore’s Law and Dennard scaling. The 1T1C DRAM cell, with one access transistor and one storage capacitor, scaled for decades. Shrinking transistors reduced cost per bit, while clever capacitor engineering preserved sufficient charge to maintain signal integrity.

pro.text: 自 20 世纪 70 年代投入商业化应用以来，DRAM 一直受益于定义半导体行业的两大缩放定律：摩尔定律与登纳德缩放定律。包含一个存取晶体管和一个存储电容的 1T1C DRAM 存储单元（1T1C DRAM cell），在过去几十年里持续微缩。晶体管微缩降低了每比特成本，而精妙的电容工程设计则保留了充足电荷，确保了信号完整性。

applied_terms: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

understanding.role_in_article: Provides historical and technical context to explain the structural shift in the DRAM industry from technology-driven deflation to capacity-driven cyclicality.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Fulfills the promise made at the end of the intro to provide 'background' before diving into current market dynamics.
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the physical limitations (slowing density gains) that directly cause the market behaviors and capital indiscipline discussed next.
key_points: DRAM historically scaled via Moore's Law and Dennard scaling using the 1T1C cell structure.
As density gains hit physical limits, the traditional deflationary force of technology-driven cost-per-bit reduction has slowed.
DRAM pricing is now dictated primarily by capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics.
translation_notes: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

issues.description: 译文将 'commoditized product' 译为 '高度商品化的产品'。根据翻译指南，半导体语境下的 'Commodity'（如 Commodity DRAM）应译为 '标准型' 或 '大宗'，以符合行业标准术语。
译文为 '电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1'。译者在此处超译了 '极高深宽比'，与后文的 '深宽比逼近 100:1' 产生了语义重复，且原文仅为 'extreme three-dimensional structures'。

paragraphs.source: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: For much of the industry’s history, DRAM density scaled faster than logic, doubling roughly every 18 months instead of 24 months and driving dramatic cost reductions. As a commoditized product, manufacturers needed to sustain cost-per-bit declines to stay competitive. Suppliers who couldn’t compete on cost fell into a downward spiral: low sales left them short on cash to finance next-generation nodes, which in turn left them further behind on cost-per-bit. Many DRAM producers fell victim and went into bankruptcy, resulting in consolidation to just a few major players today.

pro.text: 在行业发展的大部分时间里，DRAM 密度的微缩速度远超逻辑芯片，大约每 18 个月（而非 24 个月）翻一番，推动成本大幅下降。作为高度商品化的产品，制造商必须持续压低每比特成本，才能维持竞争力。无法在成本上竞争的供应商陷入恶性循环：销量低迷导致现金流枯竭，无力投资下一代节点；这又反过来导致其在每比特成本上进一步落后。众多 DRAM 生产商因此沦为牺牲品并走向破产，行业历经整合，最终演变为如今仅剩几家巨头鼎立的格局。

paragraphs.source: Yet DRAM scaling has slowed significantly over the past few decades, and density gains over time have shrunk. Over the past decade, DRAM density has increased by only ~2× in total, versus roughly ~100× per decade during the industry’s peak scaling era. Capacitors are now extreme three-dimensional structures with aspect ratios approaching 100:1, storing just tens of thousands of electrons. For comparison, a small static shock when you touch a metal doorknob might involve the transfer of billions of electrons. The static charge on just a speck of dust might be 10,000x what is stored in a modern DRAM cell.

For comparison, a small static shock when you touch a metal doorknob might involve the transfer of billions of electrons. The static charge on just a speck of dust might be 10,000x what is stored in a modern DRAM cell.

pro.text: 然而，过去几十年间 DRAM 的微缩步伐已显著放缓，密度随时间提升的幅度也在不断萎缩。过去十年里，DRAM 密度总共仅提升了约 2 倍；而在行业微缩的巅峰时期，每十年的提升幅度高达约 100 倍。如今，电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1，内部仅存储数万个电子。相比之下，当你触碰金属门把手时产生的微弱静电冲击，就可能涉及数十亿个电子的转移。仅仅一粒灰尘上携带的静电电荷，就可能是现代 DRAM 存储单元所存电荷的 10000 倍。

paragraphs.source: An easy way to understand the technical challenge in DRAM scaling is to think of a DRAM cell as a tiny bucket that holds electricity instead of water. Each bucket stores a bit of data by holding a small electrical charge. Over the years, engineers made these buckets smaller to fit more memory on a chip. At first this worked well. But today, those buckets are not just tall they are tall and narrow, each is like a tiny drinking straw standing upright. Because of the size each bucket now holds very very few electrons.

But today, those buckets are not just tall they are tall and narrow, each is like a tiny drinking straw standing upright. Because of the size each bucket now holds very very few electrons.

pro.text: 要理解 DRAM 微缩面临的技术挑战，不妨将 DRAM 存储单元想象成一个装电而非装水的小桶。每个小桶通过留存微量电荷来存储一比特数据。多年来，工程师不断缩小这些小桶的体积，以便在单块芯片上塞入更多内存。这在初期成效显著。然而时至今日，这些小桶不仅变得极高，而且又高又窄，简直就像一根根直立的极细吸管。受制于物理尺寸，如今每个小桶所能容纳的电子数量已寥寥无几。

paragraphs.source: This is a problem. When the system tries to read the data, it has to detect this very faint electrical signal and distinguish it from noise. The wires that connect these cells (the “bitline”) and the tiny sensors that read them (called sense amplifiers) are now the main bottleneck. The signal is so weak that even small variations in manufacturing or temperature can cause errors.

pro.text: 问题随之而来。当系统尝试读取数据时，必须捕捉这种极其微弱的电信号，并将其与背景噪声区分开来。连接这些存储单元的导线（即“位线”）以及负责读取信号的微型传感器（即“读出放大器”）如今已成为核心瓶颈。由于信号过于微弱，哪怕是制造工艺或温度出现极小波动，都可能引发数据错误。

paragraphs.source: Together, these constraints explain why DRAM density has stagnated and why DRAM scaling has slowed down significantly over the years. The collapse of DRAM scaling has far-reaching consequences across cost, architecture, and industry structure.

paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Together, these constraints explain why DRAM density has stagnated and why DRAM scaling has slowed down significantly over the years. The collapse of DRAM scaling has far-reaching consequences across cost, architecture, and industry structure.

pro.text: 种种物理限制叠加，解释了为何 DRAM 密度陷入停滞，以及为何其微缩步伐在过去数年间大幅放缓。DRAM 微缩进程的崩塌，正对成本控制、底层架构乃至整个行业的竞争格局产生深远影响。

applied_terms: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

understanding.role_in_article: Provides historical and technical context to explain the structural shift in the DRAM industry from technology-driven deflation to capacity-driven cyclicality.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Fulfills the promise made at the end of the intro to provide 'background' before diving into current market dynamics.
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the physical limitations (slowing density gains) that directly cause the market behaviors and capital indiscipline discussed next.
key_points: DRAM historically scaled via Moore's Law and Dennard scaling using the 1T1C cell structure.
As density gains hit physical limits, the traditional deflationary force of technology-driven cost-per-bit reduction has slowed.
DRAM pricing is now dictated primarily by capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics.
translation_notes: Hardcore tech terms must be rigorously accurate: '1T1C DRAM cell' (1T1C DRAM存储单元), 'access transistor' (存取晶体管), 'storage capacitor' (存储电容), 'Dennard scaling' (登纳德缩放定律).
Translate 'deflationary force' in an economic context (通缩力量/降价驱动力).

issues.description: 译文将 'commoditized product' 译为 '高度商品化的产品'。根据翻译指南，半导体语境下的 'Commodity'（如 Commodity DRAM）应译为 '标准型' 或 '大宗'，以符合行业标准术语。
译文为 '电容已演变为极端的极高深宽比三维结构，深宽比逼近 100:1'。译者在此处超译了 '极高深宽比'，与后文的 '深宽比逼近 100:1' 产生了语义重复，且原文仅为 'extreme three-dimensional structures'。

paragraphs.source: As density gains slow, cost per bit reductions have slowed down. DRAM pricing is now more dependent on capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics rather than technology-driven cost reductions which have been a powerful deflationary force.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: As density gains slow, cost per bit reductions have slowed down. DRAM pricing is now more dependent on capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics rather than technology-driven cost reductions which have been a powerful deflationary force.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: As density gains slow, cost per bit reductions have slowed down. DRAM pricing is now more dependent on capacity additions and cyclical supply-demand dynamics rather than technology-driven cost reductions which have been a powerful deflationary force.

pro.text: 随着密度增长放缓，每比特成本的下降速度也随之放缓。过去，技术驱动的成本下降曾是一股强大的通缩力量；而如今，DRAM 的定价逻辑已发生根本转变，愈发取决于新增产能与周期性的供需博弈。

sections.title: Memory Cycle Part II: Key Features of a Cycle
sections.title_translation: 存储周期（下）：周期的关键特征

paragraphs.source: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The memory industry has been defined by commoditization, which comes with cyclicality. This outcome reflects a combination of industry-wide competitive behavior, recurring lapses in capital discipline, and the nature of DRAM scaling we explained earlier.

pro.text: 存储行业向来以大宗商品化为核心特征，周期性亦随之而来。这种局面的形成，是全行业竞争博弈、资本纪律屡遭抛弃，以及前文所述 DRAM 微缩物理本质等多重因素交织的结果。

applied_terms: Business terminology requires precision: 'commoditization' (大宗商品化/通用化), 'capital discipline' (资本纪律), 'pure bit growth' (纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长).
Ensure 'structurally tighter supply' (结构性供应趋紧) is emphasized as a core recurring theme.

understanding.role_in_article: Explains the economic and behavioral mechanics of memory cycles, highlighting how recent industry trauma led to the structurally tight supply of the current era.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shifts focus from the physical/technical constraints of DRAM scaling to its economic and behavioral consequences (commoditization).
understanding.relation_to_next: Lays the 'structurally tighter supply' baseline, which the AI/HBM factors will dramatically exacerbate in the core thesis section.
key_points: Memory cyclicality is driven by commoditization, competitive behavior, and lapses in capital discipline.
The previous cycle reset industry expectations, forcing memory suppliers to prioritize disciplined capex and high-margin products over pure bit growth.
Customers now recognize supply chain fragility, laying the foundation for a structurally tighter supply.
translation_notes: Business terminology requires precision: 'commoditization' (大宗商品化/通用化), 'capital discipline' (资本纪律), 'pure bit growth' (纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长).
Ensure 'structurally tighter supply' (结构性供应趋紧) is emphasized as a core recurring theme.

issues.description: 术语翻译不一致。本文中对“bit”的翻译在“比特”与“位元”之间反复横跳（如同一段落出现“比特售价”与“位元供应”，以及第7、8、9、46段均使用了“位元”）。此外，系统提示已明确要求将“pure bit growth”译为“纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长”，术语表也规定了“Bit output → 比特产出”。
将知名半导体市场研究机构“Integrated Circuit Engineering (ICE)”直译为了“集成电路（integrated circuit）工程公司”。
“greenfield”在半导体与制造业语境中指“全新厂址建设/绿地项目”，译文处理为“新建（绿地）”略显生硬，带有翻译腔。
中英文之间缺少必要的空格（如“1c节点”、“1a节点”）。

paragraphs.source: At its core, memory’s cyclicality is driven by timing mismatches between demand changes and corresponding supply responses. Aside from the buffer of short-term inventories, DRAM supply is not very flexible. It can take years to bring meaningful new DRAM supply online, trying to meet demand that fluctuates daily.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: At its core, memory’s cyclicality is driven by timing mismatches between demand changes and corresponding supply responses. Aside from the buffer of short-term inventories, DRAM supply is not very flexible. It can take years to bring meaningful new DRAM supply online, trying to meet demand that fluctuates daily.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: At its core, memory’s cyclicality is driven by timing mismatches between demand changes and corresponding supply responses. Aside from the buffer of short-term inventories, DRAM supply is not very flexible. It can take years to bring meaningful new DRAM supply online, trying to meet demand that fluctuates daily.

pro.text: 究其根本，存储周期的驱动力在于需求变化与供应响应之间的时间错配。若抛开短期库存的缓冲作用不谈，DRAM 的供应弹性极低。要让实质性的新增 DRAM 产能上线往往耗时数年，而市场需求却瞬息万变，二者极难同频。

paragraphs.source: Memory manufacturing, much like logic, is among the most capital-intensive industries in the world. Building leading-edge DRAM and NAND fabs requires multi-billion-dollar investments (which have steadily increased over the past few decades), multi-year construction timelines, extended yield-learning curves across successive process nodes, and lengthy ramp-up periods before meaningful volume production is achieved.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Memory manufacturing, much like logic, is among the most capital-intensive industries in the world. Building leading-edge DRAM and NAND fabs requires multi-billion-dollar investments (which have steadily increased over the past few decades), multi-year construction timelines, extended yield-learning curves across successive process nodes, and lengthy ramp-up periods before meaningful volume production is achieved.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Memory manufacturing, much like logic, is among the most capital-intensive industries in the world. Building leading-edge DRAM and NAND fabs requires multi-billion-dollar investments (which have steadily increased over the past few decades), multi-year construction timelines, extended yield-learning curves across successive process nodes, and lengthy ramp-up periods before meaningful volume production is achieved.

pro.text: 与逻辑芯片类似，存储制造稳居全球资本最密集行业之列。建设先进制程的 DRAM 和 NAND 晶圆厂需要砸下数十亿美元（且该数字在过去数十年间持续攀升）。不仅建设周期长达数年，跨代制程节点的良率学习曲线极为漫长，在实现实质性量产前，还需经历旷日持久的产能爬坡期。

paragraphs.source: These heavy capital investments mean that suppliers need to operate with high utilization rates to generate cash profits to earn a payback. However, utilization rates ultimately depend on market demand, which is influenced by a range of external factors (macroeconomy, end market sentiment, product cycle, etc). In practice, utilization can swing dramatically across cycles, ranging from roughly 95% in a supercycle to as low as 50% in significant downcycles.

pro.text: 如此沉重的资本开支，意味着供应商必须维持高利用率运转，方能创造现金利润以收回投资。然而，利用率最终取决于市场需求，而需求又受制于诸多外部因素（宏观经济、终端市场情绪、产品周期等）。在实际运作中，利用率在不同周期内波动剧烈：在超级周期中可飙升至 95% 左右，而在严重的下行周期中则会一路陡降至 50%。

paragraphs.source: Still, because the majority of the cost is already sunk, the fab is built and equipment purchased, suppliers are better off running wafers so long as they can sell bits above cash operating costs. Where demand is weaker than bit supply, prices go down as you’d expect. Elasticity within the market is always a hot debate.

pro.text: 尽管如此，由于大部分成本已沦为沉没成本（晶圆厂已落成，设备已就位），只要比特售价能覆盖现金运营成本，供应商继续流片生产晶圆就是更优解。当需求不及比特供应时，价格自然应声下跌。至于市场内部的需求弹性，则历来是业内争论的焦点。

paragraphs.source: Memory supply can be expanded by migrating to more advanced process nodes with yield improvements, which increases bit supply without requiring new “greenfield” wafer capacity additions. For example, Samsung’s leading-edge 1c DRAM process node delivers roughly ~70% higher bits output per wafer compared with its 1a node. This means that on a per-wafer basis, a 1c-node DRAM wafer can deliver roughly 70% higher bit output than a 1a-node wafer (assuming the same yield), meaning significantly more memory supply from the same amount of raw material.

pro.text: 供应商还可通过向更先进制程节点迭代并提升良率来扩张存储供应。这种方式无需投建全新的“绿地”晶圆产能，即可直接提升比特供应。以三星（Samsung）为例，其最前沿的 1c 节点 DRAM 制程与 1a 节点相比，单片晶圆的比特产出提升了约 70%。这意味着在同等良率假设下，单片 1c 节点 DRAM 晶圆的比特产出比 1a 节点高出约 70%；换言之，消耗同等规模的原材料，却能产出丰厚得多的存储供应。

paragraphs.source: The impact of node migration, however, is dynamic over time. When a new node is first introduced, initial yields are typically lower, limiting effective output as well as wafer capacity, given the potential new equipment introduction, replacement, and ramp-up timeline. As yield learning progresses and node migration expands, the bit output per wafer increases materially, resulting in more bit supply even if wafer output is held constant.

When a new node is first introduced, initial yields are typically lower, limiting effective output as well as wafer capacity, given the potential new equipment introduction, replacement, and ramp-up timeline. pro.text: 然而，节点演进的影响会随时间动态变化。首次引入新制程节点时，初期良率通常较低。考虑到潜在的新设备引入、替换以及产能爬坡时间表，这会限制有效产出与晶圆产能。随着良率不断改善且节点演进深入，单片晶圆的比特产出将大幅提升；此时即使晶圆产出保持不变，也会带来更多位元供应。

paragraphs.source: In a downturn, the impact of pricing declines can be existential for memory suppliers. By the time pricing rolls over, manufacturers have already committed and deployed multi-billion-dollar capital investments into fabs and equipment that cannot be economically idled. As demand weakens, utilization rates fall, fixed costs are under-absorbed, and cash generation deteriorates rapidly. The result is a sharp compression in gross margins and an inability to earn an adequate return on invested capital precisely when balance-sheet stress is rising.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: In a downturn, the impact of pricing declines can be existential for memory suppliers. By the time pricing rolls over, manufacturers have already committed and deployed multi-billion-dollar capital investments into fabs and equipment that cannot be economically idled. As demand weakens, utilization rates fall, fixed costs are under-absorbed, and cash generation deteriorates rapidly. The result is a sharp compression in gross margins and an inability to earn an adequate return on invested capital precisely when balance-sheet stress is rising.

As demand weakens, utilization rates fall, fixed costs are under-absorbed, and cash generation deteriorates rapidly. The result is a sharp compression in gross margins and an inability to earn an adequate return on invested capital precisely when balance-sheet stress is rising.

pro.text: 步入下行周期后，价格暴跌对存储供应商而言往往是生死攸关的。当价格见顶回落时，制造商早已砸下数十亿美元的资本支出（capex），将其部署在晶圆厂与设备上；强行闲置这些资产极不划算。随着需求疲软，利用率随之下滑，固定成本未充分吸收，现金流创造能力迅速恶化。最终结果是毛利率急剧收缩，且恰逢资产负债表压力飙升之际，企业根本无法获得足够的投资资本回报率。

paragraphs.source: During the Windows PC supercycle of the early to mid-1990s, there were approximately 20 meaningful DRAM suppliers. Elevated demand and strong pricing attracted aggressive capital investment and new entrants. Subsequent downcycles systematically eliminated the weaker players. From roughly 20+ players in the mid-1990s, the number of players contracted to the mid-teens in the 2000s and early 2010s, to fewer than 10 relevant suppliers in the 2020s. Today, there are only 3-4 material suppliers.

pro.text: 在二十世纪九十年代初中期的 Windows 个人电脑 (PC) 超级周期期间，市场上约有 20 家具备规模的 DRAM 供应商。高涨的需求与强劲的价格吸引了激进的资本投资与新入局者。随后的下行周期对弱势玩家进行了系统性出清。从九十年代中期的 20 多家厂商，到本世纪初及 2010 年代初萎缩至十五六家，再到 2020 年代相关供应商已不足 10 家。时至今日，真正具备影响力的供应商仅剩 3 到 4 家。

paragraphs.source: From a demand perspective, memory consumption is not always linear or predictable. During mature phases of existing product cycles, demand growth can be relatively stable, driven primarily by incremental unit growth or stable increases in memory content per device. However, during “inflection periods,” when new computing platforms or architectures emerge as primary demand drivers, memory demand can shift abruptly. During these periods, memory consumption tends to grow non-linearly if not explosively.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: From a demand perspective, memory consumption is not always linear or predictable. During mature phases of existing product cycles, demand growth can be relatively stable, driven primarily by incremental unit growth or stable increases in memory content per device. However, during “inflection periods,” when new computing platforms or architectures emerge as primary demand drivers, memory demand can shift abruptly. During these periods, memory consumption tends to grow non-linearly if not explosively.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: From a demand perspective, memory consumption is not always linear or predictable. During mature phases of existing product cycles, demand growth can be relatively stable, driven primarily by incremental unit growth or stable increases in memory content per device. However, during “inflection periods,” when new computing platforms or architectures emerge as primary demand drivers, memory demand can shift abruptly. During these periods, memory consumption tends to grow non-linearly if not explosively.

pro.text: 从需求端来看，存储消耗并不总是线性或可预测的。在现有产品周期的成熟阶段，需求增长相对平稳，主要由终端出货量的增量或单机存储容量的稳步提升来驱动。然而，一旦进入“拐点期”——即全新计算平台或架构跃升为核心需求驱动力时，存储需求便会发生剧变。在此期间，存储消耗往往呈非线性甚至爆炸式增长。

paragraphs.source: There have been several of these product cycle inflections over the past few decades. New computing platforms like the PC, the smartphone, cloud computing, and now AI accelerators drove abrupt increases in both the number of systems and memory content per system. In prior cycles, these demand inflections often caught memory suppliers off guard, a dynamic we will discuss in the following section.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: There have been several of these product cycle inflections over the past few decades. New computing platforms like the PC, the smartphone, cloud computing, and now AI accelerators drove abrupt increases in both the number of systems and memory content per system. In prior cycles, these demand inflections often caught memory suppliers off guard, a dynamic we will discuss in the following section.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: There have been several of these product cycle inflections over the past few decades. New computing platforms like the PC, the smartphone, cloud computing, and now AI accelerators drove abrupt increases in both the number of systems and memory content per system. In prior cycles, these demand inflections often caught memory suppliers off guard, a dynamic we will discuss in the following section.

pro.text: 过去几十年间，这类产品周期拐点曾多次出现。PC、智能手机、云计算以及如今的 AI 加速器等全新计算平台，双重驱动了系统数量与单机存储容量的陡增。在过往周期中，这些需求拐点往往让存储供应商措手不及，我们将在下一节深入探讨这一动态。

paragraphs.source: However, such inflection-driven upcycles have not been sustainable in the long run. Prior memory supercycles have tended to peak and roll into downcycles within one or two years, as elevated profitability drives aggressive capital investment, accelerated capacity expansion, and faster-than-anticipated bit supply growth. These supply responses, combined with the inherently cyclical nature of end demand, have consistently led to oversupply and subsequent market corrections.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: However, such inflection-driven upcycles have not been sustainable in the long run. Prior memory supercycles have tended to peak and roll into downcycles within one or two years, as elevated profitability drives aggressive capital investment, accelerated capacity expansion, and faster-than-anticipated bit supply growth. These supply responses, combined with the inherently cyclical nature of end demand, have consistently led to oversupply and subsequent market corrections.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: However, such inflection-driven upcycles have not been sustainable in the long run. Prior memory supercycles have tended to peak and roll into downcycles within one or two years, as elevated profitability drives aggressive capital investment, accelerated capacity expansion, and faster-than-anticipated bit supply growth. These supply responses, combined with the inherently cyclical nature of end demand, have consistently led to oversupply and subsequent market corrections.

pro.text: 然而，从长远来看，这类由拐点驱动的上行周期往往难以持久。过往的存储超级周期通常在一两年内便会触顶并滑入下行周期，因为高企的利润会刺激激进的资本投资、加速的产能扩张以及超预期的位元供应增长。这些供应端反应，叠加终端需求固有的周期性特质，必然导致供过于求及随之而来的市场回调。

paragraphs.source: There are a few especially interesting examples, worth revisiting in the context of the current memory supercycle: the 1993 Windows PC supercycle, the cloud and mobile upcycle in 2010, the 2017-2018 supercycle from cloud and NAND, and the unexpected upcycle during Covid-19.

paragraphs.source: During the 1993 memory supercycle, the DRAM industry entered a strong upcycle driven by the rapid adoption of Windows PCs and the broad implementation of graphical operating systems. Unlike prior generations of personal computers, Windows PCs transitioned from text-based interfaces to GUI (Graphical User Interface)-driven computing, which dramatically increased DRAM requirements per system. Average DRAM content per PC jumped from roughly 1–2MB to 4–8MB, representing an approximate 4× increase in memory content per device. This step-function increase in DRAM intensity coincided with accelerating PC adoption, with unit shipments growing at roughly double-digit rates.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: During the 1993 memory supercycle, the DRAM industry entered a strong upcycle driven by the rapid adoption of Windows PCs and the broad implementation of graphical operating systems. Unlike prior generations of personal computers, Windows PCs transitioned from text-based interfaces to GUI (Graphical User Interface)-driven computing, which dramatically increased DRAM requirements per system. Average DRAM content per PC jumped from roughly 1–2MB to 4–8MB, representing an approximate 4× increase in memory content per device. This step-function increase in DRAM intensity coincided with accelerating PC adoption, with unit shipments growing at roughly double-digit rates.

This step-function increase in DRAM intensity coincided with accelerating PC adoption, with unit shipments growing at roughly double-digit rates.

pro.text: 在 1993 年的存储超级周期中，随着 Windows PC 的快速普及与图形操作系统的广泛应用，DRAM 行业步入强劲的上行周期。与前几代个人电脑不同，Windows PC 从基于文本的界面转向由图形用户界面 (GUI) 驱动的计算模式，这使得单机 DRAM 需求激增。每台 PC 的平均 DRAM 容量从约 1 至 2MB 跃升至 4 至 8MB，单机存储容量实现了约 4 倍的增长。DRAM 搭载量的这种阶梯式跃升，恰逢 PC 普及加速，系统出货量也保持着约两位数的增速。

paragraphs.source: On the supply side, the industry was emerging from a prolonged downturn in the late 1980s because of intensified competition, declining margins, and a significant shakeout among U.S. and European DRAM suppliers. As a result, capacity expansion had been constrained and yields were uneven, leaving the supply suppliers ill-prepared to absorb the sudden surge in demand. With this perfect setup in both supply and demand, an industry shortage inevitably occurred. During 1993 and 1994, DRAM demand outpaced supply despite most fabs running at full utilization. Spot and contract prices for 4Mb and 16Mb DRAM rose sharply, and gross margins for leading suppliers surged well above 50%.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: On the supply side, the industry was emerging from a prolonged downturn in the late 1980s because of intensified competition, declining margins, and a significant shakeout among U.S. and European DRAM suppliers. As a result, capacity expansion had been constrained and yields were uneven, leaving the supply suppliers ill-prepared to absorb the sudden surge in demand. With this perfect setup in both supply and demand, an industry shortage inevitably occurred. During 1993 and 1994, DRAM demand outpaced supply despite most fabs running at full utilization. Spot and contract prices for 4Mb and 16Mb DRAM rose sharply, and gross margins for leading suppliers surged well above 50%.

With this perfect setup in both supply and demand, an industry shortage inevitably occurred. During 1993 and 1994, DRAM demand outpaced supply despite most fabs running at full utilization. pro.text: 在供应端，由于竞争白热化、利润率下滑，加之欧美 DRAM 供应商经历了一轮大洗牌，整个行业在 20 世纪 80 年代末刚从漫长的低谷期中复苏。因此，产能扩张处处受限，良率参差不齐，导致供应商面对突如其来的需求激增时措手不及。供需两端如此完美的共振，令全行业的短缺成为必然。1993 至 1994 年间，尽管大多数晶圆厂都在满负荷运转，DRAM 依然供不应求。4Mb 和 16Mb DRAM 的现货与合约价格一路狂飙，头部供应商的毛利率更是飙升至 50% 以上。

paragraphs.source: Unsurprisingly, this set the stage for a downturn. Japanese incumbents and then-emerging Korean players responded with aggressive capacity expansion—bringing new fabs online and accelerating process shrinks. This was reflected in global semiconductor manufacturing spend as the supercycle took hold, with capex as a percentage of semiconductor production rising steadily and at one point exceeding 30%. Reinforcing this late-cycle signal, roughly 50 fab construction plans were announced during 1995–1996 alone.

pro.text: 毫无意外，这也为随后的下行周期埋下了伏笔。日本老牌巨头与当时正崛起的韩国厂商纷纷以激进的产能扩张予以回应——新晶圆厂密集投产，工艺微缩进程全面提速。随着超级周期步入高潮，全球半导体制造支出也印证了这一点：资本支出占半导体产值的比例稳步攀升，一度突破 30%。仅在 1995 至 1996 年间，业内就宣布了约 50 项晶圆厂建设计划，进一步坐实了这一周期尾声的危险信号。

paragraphs.source: Rapid yield improvements further amplified supply, driving a sharp increase in bits per wafer. By 1995–1996, the market gradually flipped from shortage to oversupply. This led to sharp price declines of more than 60%, forcing widespread exits and accelerating industry consolidation.

pro.text: 良率的快速提升进一步放大了供应量，单片晶圆的比特产出急剧增长。到了 1995 至 1996 年间，市场逐步从短缺反转为供过于求。这导致价格一路陡降，跌幅超过 60%，迫使大量厂商黯然退场，同时加速了全行业的兼并重组。

paragraphs.source: The 2010 memory supercycle started in roughly the 2nd half of 2009, driven by two simultaneous demand inflections: (1) the smartphone rapid adoption globally, led by the iPhone and a rapidly proliferating Android ecosystem, and (2) the early hyperscaler buildout wave (e.g., Google, Amazon, Facebook). Server DRAM intensity increased due to virtualization and scale-out services. Supply growth was muted as suppliers drastically cut investment in the poor macro environment post the Global Financial Crisis.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The 2010 memory supercycle started in roughly the 2nd half of 2009, driven by two simultaneous demand inflections: (1) the smartphone rapid adoption globally, led by the iPhone and a rapidly proliferating Android ecosystem, and (2) the early hyperscaler buildout wave (e.g., Google, Amazon, Facebook). Server DRAM intensity increased due to virtualization and scale-out services. Supply growth was muted as suppliers drastically cut investment in the poor macro environment post the Global Financial Crisis.

Server DRAM intensity increased due to virtualization and scale-out services. Supply growth was muted as suppliers drastically cut investment in the poor macro environment post the Global Financial Crisis.

pro.text:
2010 年的存储器超级周期大约始于 2009 年下半年，受两大需求拐点双重驱动：
1. 由 iPhone 及迅速扩张的 Android 生态系统引领的全球智能手机快速普及
2. 早期超大规模云服务商（如谷歌、亚马逊、Facebook）的建设浪潮
受虚拟化和横向扩展服务推动，服务器 DRAM 密度不断提升。同时，在全球金融危机后的恶劣宏观环境下，供应商大幅削减投资，导致供应增长疲软。

paragraphs.source: On top of this, smartphone demand growth was a near-vertical volume step-up. Global smartphonec by broader Android price-tier penetration and iPhone scaling. On the cloud side, while the servers’ shipment growth increased much more slowly compared to mobile during that period, the key change was the DRAM content per server. The industry was shifting toward higher-memory configurations to support consolidation, virtualization density, and early big-data workloads. DRAM content per server increased from single-digit gigabytes (GBs) to tens of GBs, representing a significant step-function increase in memory intensity per system.

pro.text: 在此之上，智能手机需求增长呈现近乎垂直的阶梯式跃升。随着 Android 阵营在各价位段的广泛渗透以及 iPhone 规模的不断扩大，全球智能手机销量激增。在云端，尽管该时期服务器出货量的增速远不及移动设备，但核心变化在于单台服务器的 DRAM 容量。整个行业正向更高内存配置演进，以支持资源整合、虚拟化密度以及早期的大数据工作负载。单台服务器的 DRAM 容量从个位数吉字节（GB）飙升至数十 GB，标志着单系统内存密度的显著阶梯式跃升。

Customers now recognize supply chain fragility, laying the foundation for a structurally tighter supply.

paragraphs.source: Similar to prior cycles, the sudden demand surge combined with constrained supply drove a meaningful increase in DRAM pricing. What differentiated the 2010 cycle, however, was the rapid rise of LPDDR within the overall DRAM mix as mobile demand scaled. Because LPDDR products are mostly more cost- and price-efficient and are sold into more price-sensitive end markets, the resulting pricing uplift was materially less dramatic than prior PC-driven supercycles. The pricing characteristics of LPDDR have remained structurally consistent to this day.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Similar to prior cycles, the sudden demand surge combined with constrained supply drove a meaningful increase in DRAM pricing. What differentiated the 2010 cycle, however, was the rapid rise of LPDDR within the overall DRAM mix as mobile demand scaled. Because LPDDR products are mostly more cost- and price-efficient and are sold into more price-sensitive end markets, the resulting pricing uplift was materially less dramatic than prior PC-driven supercycles. The pricing characteristics of LPDDR have remained structurally consistent to this day.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Similar to prior cycles, the sudden demand surge combined with constrained supply drove a meaningful increase in DRAM pricing. What differentiated the 2010 cycle, however, was the rapid rise of LPDDR within the overall DRAM mix as mobile demand scaled. Because LPDDR products are mostly more cost- and price-efficient and are sold into more price-sensitive end markets, the resulting pricing uplift was materially less dramatic than prior PC-driven supercycles. The pricing characteristics of LPDDR have remained structurally consistent to this day.

pro.text: 与以往周期类似，需求骤增与供应受限双重作用，推动 DRAM 价格大幅上涨。然而，2010 年周期的不同之处在于，随着移动端需求爆发，LPDDR 在整体 DRAM 结构中的占比迅速攀升。由于 LPDDR 产品大多在成本和定价上更具效率，且面向对价格更敏感的终端市场，这导致其价格涨幅明显不及此前由个人电脑 (PC) 驱动的超级周期。LPDDR 的这一价格特征在结构上一直延续至今。

paragraphs.source: Moreover, DRAM pricing in this cycle peaked earlier and rolled over more quickly than in previous upcycles. Pricing momentum proved difficult to sustain even within the nominal upcycle window. The DDR3 2Gb contract pricing fell ~46% from its 1H10 peak of ~$46.5 to ~$25 by November 2010. By 2011, the correction had broadened further, with a further correction of DRAM pricing.

Pricing momentum proved difficult to sustain even within the nominal upcycle window. The DDR3 2Gb contract pricing fell ~46% from its 1H10 peak of ~$46.5 to ~$25 by November 2010. pro.text: 此外，本轮周期中 DRAM 价格见顶更早，且回落速度快于以往的上行周期。即使在名义上的上行周期窗口内，价格上涨动能也难以维系。DDR3 2Gb 合约价从 2010 年上半年的约 46.5 美元高点，暴跌约 46% 至 2010 年 11 月的约 25 美元。进入 2011 年，回调范围进一步扩大，DRAM 价格持续下探。

paragraphs.source: Another key development of this cycle was the rapid standardization of mobile DRAM which further accelerated commoditization. LPDDR2 was standardized by JEDEC in 2009, pushing mobile DRAM into a tightly specified, consumer-oriented market structure that limited supplier differentiation and therefore pricing power. These factors together only compressed the length of the upcycles and time to reach the downcycle starting roughly in 2nd half of 2010 and following years.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Another key development of this cycle was the rapid standardization of mobile DRAM which further accelerated commoditization. LPDDR2 was standardized by JEDEC in 2009, pushing mobile DRAM into a tightly specified, consumer-oriented market structure that limited supplier differentiation and therefore pricing power. These factors together only compressed the length of the upcycles and time to reach the downcycle starting roughly in 2nd half of 2010 and following years.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Another key development of this cycle was the rapid standardization of mobile DRAM which further accelerated commoditization. LPDDR2 was standardized by JEDEC in 2009, pushing mobile DRAM into a tightly specified, consumer-oriented market structure that limited supplier differentiation and therefore pricing power. These factors together only compressed the length of the upcycles and time to reach the downcycle starting roughly in 2nd half of 2010 and following years.

pro.text: 本轮周期的另一项关键进展是移动端 DRAM 的快速标准化，这进一步加速了其通用化进程。2009 年，JEDEC (固态技术协会) 制定了 LPDDR2 标准，将移动端 DRAM 推入规格严苛、面向消费者的市场结构，限制了供应商的差异化空间，进而削弱了其定价权。这些因素共同压缩了上行周期的长度，并缩短了进入下行周期的时间，导致下行周期在大约 2010 年下半年及随后几年提前到来。

paragraphs.source: The 2017–2018 memory supercycle is still a fresh memory for many in the industry and is oft-cited as a good analogue for today’s supercycle. On the demand side, the key driver was server upgrades and buildouts, driven by virtualization, scale-out architectures, and increasingly memory-intensive workloads. DRAM content per server increased materially, shifting demand toward higher-capacity configurations. Server DRAM also enjoys higher ASP and margins compared to PC and mobile, and is therefore more profitable for suppliers.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The 2017–2018 memory supercycle is still a fresh memory for many in the industry and is oft-cited as a good analogue for today’s supercycle. On the demand side, the key driver was server upgrades and buildouts, driven by virtualization, scale-out architectures, and increasingly memory-intensive workloads. DRAM content per server increased materially, shifting demand toward higher-capacity configurations. Server DRAM also enjoys higher ASP and margins compared to PC and mobile, and is therefore more profitable for suppliers.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The 2017–2018 memory supercycle is still a fresh memory for many in the industry and is oft-cited as a good analogue for today’s supercycle. On the demand side, the key driver was server upgrades and buildouts, driven by virtualization, scale-out architectures, and increasingly memory-intensive workloads. DRAM content per server increased materially, shifting demand toward higher-capacity configurations. Server DRAM also enjoys higher ASP and margins compared to PC and mobile, and is therefore more profitable for suppliers.

pro.text: 业内许多人对 2017 至 2018 年的存储器超级周期依然记忆犹新，该周期也常被视为当前超级周期的绝佳参照。在需求端，核心驱动力在于服务器的升级与扩建，这主要受虚拟化、横向扩展架构以及内存密集度日益增加的工作负载所推动。单台服务器的 DRAM 容量大幅增加，促使需求向大容量配置转移。相较于 PC 和移动端，服务器 DRAM 享有更高的平均售价 (ASP) 和利润率，因此能为供应商带来更丰厚的利润。

paragraphs.source: This pricing environment translated directly into record financial performance, peaking in 2H18. Memory suppliers generated unprecedented levels of free cash flow, and gross margins expanded to levels previously thought unattainable for a commoditized industry.

pro.text: 这种定价环境直接转化为创纪录的财务业绩，于 2018 年下半年触及顶峰。存储器供应商创造了史无前例的自由现金流，毛利率也一路飙升，达到了此前在这个高度标准化的行业中被认为遥不可及的水平。

paragraphs.source: Yet, as with the prior cycles, the fundamental cyclical dynamics of the industry prevailed. A few familiar factors developed in late 2018 and 2019: (1) supply growth re-accelerated as manufacturers responded to elevated pricing with aggressive capacity additions and technology ramps, and (2) demand growth normalized as end markets absorbed excess inventory and hyperscaler purchasing slowed. Oversupply drove pricing down, and the cycle spiraled downwards.

然而，与以往周期一样，行业底层的周期性规律依然发挥了主导作用。2018 年底至 2019 年，几个熟悉的因素再度浮现：
(1) 面对高企的价格，制造商激进扩充产能并加速技术爬坡，导致供应增长重新加速；
(2) 随着终端市场消化过剩库存，加上超大规模云服务商采购放缓，需求增长回归常态。
供过于求迫使价格下行，整个周期随之螺旋式坠落。

paragraphs.source: The most recent DRAM upcycle was catalyzed during the COVID-era “chip crisis,” an unprecedented synchronization of demand shock and supply disruption. Global lockdowns caused abrupt shifts in end markets, including work-from-home, remote education, cloud usage, and digital entertainment. These unexpected social changes drove a sudden surge in demand for PCs, servers, networking equipment, and consumer electronics. All of these categories are DRAM-intensive, and no pre-pandemic forecast could have reasonably foreseen the changes.

pro.text: 距离当前最近的一次 DRAM 上行周期，是由新冠疫情时期的“芯片危机”催生的。这是一场史无前例的需求冲击与供应中断的同步爆发。全球封锁导致终端市场需求突变，居家办公、远程教育、云服务及数字娱乐全面爆发。这些始料未及的社会变革，直接引爆了个人电脑、服务器、网络设备和消费电子产品的需求狂潮。上述所有品类均高度依赖 DRAM，而疫情前的任何预测都绝无可能预见到这番剧变。

paragraphs.source: Beyond surging end-demand, purchasing behavior amplified the cycle, especially from the enterprise side. OEMs, hyperscalers, and channel partners were taking a defensive posture, aggressively placing years’ worth of orders to secure supply amidst uncertainty. This led to widespread double- and triple-ordering across the supply chain.

pro.text: 除终端需求激增外，采购行为（尤其是企业端）进一步放大了周期波动。面对高度的不确定性，OEM、超大规模云服务商以及渠道合作伙伴纷纷采取防御姿态，激进地下达长达数年的订单以锁定供应。这直接导致整个供应链中充斥着双倍甚至三倍重复下单的乱象。

The result was a rapid drawdown of industry inventories and a sharp tightening of spot and contract markets, pushing DRAM prices materially higher at their 2021 peak.

paragraphs.source: On the supply side, the industry was structurally constrained. During the pandemic, suppliers faced additional friction from labor shortages, logistics disruptions, and delayed equipment deliveries. Even where capex budgets existed, wafer output could not be ramped quickly. At the same time, most suppliers had entered the pandemic period with a relatively disciplined supply posture following the painful 2018–2019 downturn, limiting their ability and willingness to add more capacity in response to short-term pricing signals.

pro.text: 供应端同样面临结构性受限。疫情期间，劳动力短缺、物流中断以及设备延期交付给供应商带来了额外的摩擦成本。即便手握资本支出预算，晶圆产量也无法迅速爬坡。与此同时，在经历了 2018 至 2019 年惨痛的行业下行后，大多数供应商在步入疫情期时，其供应策略已趋于严守纪律。这极大限制了他们根据短期价格信号盲目扩充产能的能力与意愿。

paragraphs.source: Capex decisions during this period were therefore cautious and selective. Rather than aggressive greenfield expansion, memory suppliers prioritized node migration and productivity improvements within existing fabs. Advanced-node DRAM transitions were already becoming more complex and capital-intensive, with diminishing bit-growth returns per wafer as scaling challenges increased. This meant that even elevated capex levels translated into less incremental supply than in prior cycles. The pandemic-era upcycle thus reinforced a structural shift: supply growth became increasingly constrained not just by capital discipline, but by physics and process complexity.

pro.text: 因此，这一时期的资本支出决策极为谨慎且极具选择性。存储器供应商并未采取激进的新建（绿地）扩张，而是将现有晶圆厂内的节点演进与产能优化置于首位。先进节点 DRAM 的技术过渡本就愈发复杂且属于资本密集型，随着微缩难度攀升，单片晶圆的位元增长边际收益正在不断递减。这意味着，即便维持高额的资本支出，其转化为实际新增供应的规模也远不及以往周期。疫情时期的上行周期由此彻底坐实了一项结构性转变：供应增长的瓶颈不再仅仅是资本纪律，更在于物理极限与工艺复杂度的双重掣肘。

paragraphs.source: Crucially, this cycle reset industry behavior and expectations. Memory suppliers emerged with a stronger appreciation for disciplined capex, tighter inventory management, and the value of prioritizing higher-margin products over pure bit growth. Customers, meanwhile, recognized the fragility of semiconductor supply chains and the strategic importance of securing memory capacity. These dynamics laid the foundation for the current supercycle by creating a structurally tighter supply. In this sense, the COVID-era DRAM upcycle was not just a temporary dislocation, but a formative event that reshaped the memory industry’s supply-demand balance heading into the current cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Crucially, this cycle reset industry behavior and expectations. Memory suppliers emerged with a stronger appreciation for disciplined capex, tighter inventory management, and the value of prioritizing higher-margin products over pure bit growth. Customers, meanwhile, recognized the fragility of semiconductor supply chains and the strategic importance of securing memory capacity. These dynamics laid the foundation for the current supercycle by creating a structurally tighter supply. In this sense, the COVID-era DRAM upcycle was not just a temporary dislocation, but a formative event that reshaped the memory industry’s supply-demand balance heading into the current cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Crucially, this cycle reset industry behavior and expectations. Memory suppliers emerged with a stronger appreciation for disciplined capex, tighter inventory management, and the value of prioritizing higher-margin products over pure bit growth. Customers, meanwhile, recognized the fragility of semiconductor supply chains and the strategic importance of securing memory capacity. These dynamics laid the foundation for the current supercycle by creating a structurally tighter supply. In this sense, the COVID-era DRAM upcycle was not just a temporary dislocation, but a formative event that reshaped the memory industry’s supply-demand balance heading into the current cycle.

pro.text: 至关重要的是，本轮周期彻底重塑了行业的行为模式与预期。经历洗礼后，存储器供应商更加笃信严守资本纪律与精细化库存管理的必要性，并深刻意识到优先发展高利润率产品远比单纯追求位元增长更有价值。与此同时，客户也清醒地认识到半导体供应链的脆弱性，以及锁定存储器产能的战略意义。这些博弈与转变促成了结构性供应趋紧，为当前的超级周期埋下了伏笔。从这个意义上讲，疫情时期的 DRAM 上行周期绝非一次短暂的供需错配，而是一场深远的行业重构，它彻底改写了存储器行业的供需平衡，并直接引领我们步入当前的超级周期。

applied_terms: Business terminology requires precision: 'commoditization' (大宗商品化/通用化), 'capital discipline' (资本纪律), 'pure bit growth' (纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长).
Ensure 'structurally tighter supply' (结构性供应趋紧) is emphasized as a core recurring theme.

understanding.role_in_article: Explains the economic and behavioral mechanics of memory cycles, highlighting how recent industry trauma led to the structurally tight supply of the current era.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Shifts focus from the physical/technical constraints of DRAM scaling to its economic and behavioral consequences (commoditization).
understanding.relation_to_next: Lays the 'structurally tighter supply' baseline, which the AI/HBM factors will dramatically exacerbate in the core thesis section.
key_points: Memory cyclicality is driven by commoditization, competitive behavior, and lapses in capital discipline.
The previous cycle reset industry expectations, forcing memory suppliers to prioritize disciplined capex and high-margin products over pure bit growth.
Customers now recognize supply chain fragility, laying the foundation for a structurally tighter supply.
translation_notes: Business terminology requires precision: 'commoditization' (大宗商品化/通用化), 'capital discipline' (资本纪律), 'pure bit growth' (纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长).
Ensure 'structurally tighter supply' (结构性供应趋紧) is emphasized as a core recurring theme.

issues.description: 术语翻译不一致。本文中对“bit”的翻译在“比特”与“位元”之间反复横跳（如同一段落出现“比特售价”与“位元供应”，以及第7、8、9、46段均使用了“位元”）。此外，系统提示已明确要求将“pure bit growth”译为“纯比特增长/单纯追求容量增长”，术语表也规定了“Bit output → 比特产出”。
将知名半导体市场研究机构“Integrated Circuit Engineering (ICE)”直译为了“集成电路（integrated circuit）工程公司”。
“greenfield”在半导体与制造业语境中指“全新厂址建设/绿地项目”，译文处理为“新建（绿地）”略显生硬，带有翻译腔。
中英文之间缺少必要的空格（如“1c节点”、“1a节点”）。

sections.title: AI-Driven Memory Supercycle: Bigger, Longer, and a Shortage that is Harder to Solve
sections.title_translation: AI驱动的存储超级周期：规模更大、持续更长、短缺更难缓解

paragraphs.source: For those who have lived through multiple memory cycles, the central question when it comes to this supercycle is the same: when will this cycle peak? It is natural that both investors and the supply chain remain cautious, particularly as memory stocks rally sharply over short periods. In our view, however, while there are clear similarities to prior cycles, this supercycle is shaping up to be both larger and longer in duration, driven by dynamics that are very much unique to this cycle.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: For those who have lived through multiple memory cycles, the central question when it comes to this supercycle is the same: *when will this cycle peak?* It is natural that both investors and the supply chain remain cautious, particularly as memory stocks rally sharply over short periods. In our view, however, while there are clear similarities to prior cycles, this supercycle is shaping up to be both larger and longer in duration, driven by dynamics that are very much unique to this cycle.

It is natural that both investors and the supply chain remain cautious, particularly as memory stocks rally sharply over short periods. In our view, however, while there are clear similarities to prior cycles, this supercycle is shaping up to be both larger and longer in duration, driven by dynamics that are very much unique to this cycle.

pro.text: 对于那些经历过多轮存储周期的业内人士而言，面对当前的超级周期，核心疑问依然如故：本轮周期何时见顶？ 投资者与供应链保持谨慎实属正常，尤其是在存储板块短期内急剧飙升的背景下。然而在我们看来，尽管本轮周期与以往有明显的相似之处，但在其独有动力的驱动下，这场超级周期的规模必将更为庞大，持续时间也将更为长久。

applied_terms: Maintain a strict distinction between 'wafer capacity' (晶圆产能) and 'bit output' (比特产出).
Manufacturing terms must sound native: 'execution friction' (执行摩擦/落地阻力), 'process windows' (工艺窗口), 'yields deteriorate' (良率下降/爬坡期良率折损).

understanding.role_in_article: Delivers the core thesis: explaining exactly why the AI-driven supercycle is unique, larger, and longer due to specific structural manufacturing constraints.
understanding.relation_to_previous: Builds on the baseline of tight supply by introducing the unique, exacerbating variables of the AI era (HBM wafer drain).
understanding.relation_to_next: Establishes the massive upstream supply constraints that inevitably cause the downstream price shocks detailed in the following section.
key_points: First-order impact: Reallocating wafers to HBM directly starves commodity DRAM production.
Second-order impact: HBM production suffers a massive bit output penalty, yielding 3x to 4x fewer bits per wafer than commodity DRAM.
Node migration introduces execution friction, with new tool sets and process window re-optimization causing temporary yield and capacity declines.

issues.description: 译文在绝大多数段落（如段落 11、18、23、24 等）将 Commodity DRAM 译为“大宗DRAM”，这直接违反了 Translation Guidelines 中明确要求的“Translate 'Commodity DRAM' as '标准型 DRAM'”，且与译文自身在段落 32、35 中使用的“标准型 DRAM”前后不一。此外，“大宗”与“DRAM”之间缺失了标准的中西文空格。
译文中存在多处多余的空格或空格位置错误，如“仅为 约 123kwspm”、“增至 约 331kwspm”、“实现了 超过约 2.7 倍”。
译文处理为“晶圆制造设备（WFE）资本支出（WFE capex）预测”，出现了重复的英文缩写括注。
段落 28、35、39 中，“12层HBM3E”、“8层或12层堆叠”等数字与汉字之间缺失标准的中西文空格。
将 ASIC 直接完全汉化为“专用集成电路”，而同级别的 GPU 却保留了英文缩写。

paragraphs.source: Importantly, commodity DRAM is also forecast to remain structurally tight, with an estimated ~7% supply deficit persisting through both 2026 and 2027. At this degree of imbalance, the DRAM market is confronting what we characterize as a “dual-shortage dilemma,” a dynamic we will explore in greater detail later.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Importantly, commodity DRAM is also forecast to remain structurally tight, with an estimated ~7% supply deficit persisting through both 2026 and 2027. At this degree of imbalance, the DRAM market is confronting what we characterize as a “dual-shortage dilemma,” a dynamic we will explore in greater detail later.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Importantly, commodity DRAM is also forecast to remain structurally tight, with an estimated ~7% supply deficit persisting through both 2026 and 2027. At this degree of imbalance, the DRAM market is confronting what we characterize as a “dual-shortage dilemma,” a dynamic we will explore in greater detail later.

pro.text: 重要的是，标准型 DRAM 预计也将维持结构性紧缺，约 7% 的供给缺口将贯穿 2026 与 2027 年。在这种失衡程度下，DRAM 市场正面临我们所定义的“双重短缺困境”，我们将在后文详细剖析这一动态。

paragraphs.source: This magnitude of shortage is rare in the modern history of the memory industry, both in terms of its severity and its duration. Even during the 2017–2018 memory supercycle, the supply–demand gap was closer to the mid-single-digit range, making the current environment meaningfully tighter by comparison. Moreover, this shortage is likely to persist for far longer than the prior upcycles discussed above.

pro.text: 无论从严重程度还是持续时间来看，这种量级的短缺在存储行业的现代史上都极为罕见。即使在 2017 至 2018 年的存储超级周期期间，供需缺口也仅接近中个位数区间；相比之下，当前的市场环境明显更为吃紧。此外，本轮短缺的持续时间极有可能远超前文探讨的历次上行周期。

paragraphs.source: First is the accelerating HBM/DRAM demand and HBM’s rapidly expanding capacity. The surge in compute demand is translating into a sharp increase in memory requirements over a very short period, driven by DRAM demand across the data center. This includes DDDR/LPDDR/HBM in AI servers and DDR in general-purpose servers. As a result, demand has risen meaningfully across both commodity DRAM and HBM, which both obviously will utilize a lot of industry’s capacity.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: First is the accelerating HBM/DRAM demand and HBM’s rapidly expanding capacity. The surge in compute demand is translating into a sharp increase in memory requirements over a very short period, driven by DRAM demand across the data center. This includes DDDR/LPDDR/HBM in AI servers and DDR in general-purpose servers. As a result, demand has risen meaningfully across both commodity DRAM and HBM, which both obviously will utilize a lot of industry’s capacity.

The surge in compute demand is translating into a sharp increase in memory requirements over a very short period, driven by DRAM demand across the data center. This includes DDDR/LPDDR/HBM in AI servers and DDR in general-purpose servers. As a result, demand has risen meaningfully across both commodity DRAM and HBM, which both obviously will utilize a lot of industry’s capacity.

pro.text: 首先是 HBM 与 DRAM 需求的加速增长，以及 HBM 产能的迅速扩张。算力需求的激增正转化为短期内存储需求的急剧攀升，这主要受整个数据中心 DRAM 需求的驱动。这包括 AI 服务器中的 DDR/LPDDR/HBM，以及通用服务器中的 DDR。因此，大宗DRAM 和 HBM 的需求均出现显著增长，这两者显然都将消耗大量行业产能。

paragraphs.source: Beginning around 2023, we have observed a clear and steadily accelerating trend in HBM demand, accompanied by increasingly aggressive responses from memory suppliers to expand HBM production capacity and step up investment to capture this rapidly growing compute demand. Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only ~123kwspm of wafer capacity to HBM. By year-end 2025, this figure had increased to ~331kwspm, representing more than a ~2.7× expansion in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Beginning around 2023, we have observed a clear and steadily accelerating trend in HBM demand, accompanied by increasingly aggressive responses from memory suppliers to expand HBM production capacity and step up investment to capture this rapidly growing compute demand. Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only **~123kwspm** of wafer capacity to HBM. By **year-end 2025**, this figure had increased to **~331kwspm,** representing **more than a ~2.7× expansion** in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Beginning around 2023, we have observed a clear and steadily accelerating trend in HBM demand, accompanied by increasingly aggressive responses from memory suppliers to expand HBM production capacity and step up investment to capture this rapidly growing compute demand. Exiting 2023, the three major memory suppliers had dedicated only ~123kwspm of wafer capacity to HBM. By year-end 2025, this figure had increased to ~331kwspm, representing more than a ~2.7× expansion in wafer capacity allocated to HBM over just two years.

pro.text: 大约从 2023 年开始，我们观察到 HBM 需求呈现出清晰且稳步加速的趋势。与此同时，存储供应商的反应也愈发激进，纷纷扩充 HBM 产能并加大投资，以期充分把握这一快速增长的算力需求。2023 年底时，三大存储供应商分配给 HBM 的晶圆产能仅约为 123kwspm。到2025 年底，这一数字将增至约 331kwspm，这意味着在短短两年内，分配给 HBM 的晶圆产能实现了超过约 2.7 倍的扩张。

paragraphs.source: And the capacity expansion in HBM is far from complete. We believe additional capacity expansion is already underway, with HBM wafer capacity reaching approximately 473kwspm by year-end 2026 and ~668kwspm by year-end 2027. This implies that over the next two years, HBM wafer capacity will expand by more than 2× (again), meaning the HBM wafer capacity of three memory players is going to 5x within 4 years.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: And the capacity expansion in HBM is far from complete. We believe additional capacity expansion is already underway, with HBM wafer capacity reaching approximately 473kwspm by year-end 2026 and ~668kwspm by year-end 2027. This implies that over the next two years, HBM wafer capacity will expand by more than 2× (again), meaning the HBM wafer capacity of three memory players is going to 5x within 4 years.

We believe additional capacity expansion is already underway, with HBM wafer capacity reaching approximately 473kwspm by year-end 2026 and ~668kwspm by year-end 2027. This implies that over the next two years, HBM wafer capacity will expand by more than 2× (again), meaning the HBM wafer capacity of three memory players is going to 5x within 4 years.

pro.text: 而且，HBM 的产能扩张还远未结束。我们认为，新一轮产能扩张已在进行中，预计到 2026 年底，HBM 晶圆产能将达到约 473kwspm，到 2027 年底将达到约 668kwspm。这意味着在未来两年内，HBM 晶圆产能将（再次）实现两倍以上的扩张；换言之，三大存储厂商的 HBM 晶圆产能将在四年内增长至 5 倍。

paragraphs.source: But what’s important here is not just the significant capacity expansion in HBM, but also a meaningful trade-off between HBM and commodity DRAM wafer allocation, given the limited existing capacity as well as time for incremental wafer capacity to come in the next two years

pro.text: 但此处的关键不仅在于 HBM 产能的大幅扩张，更在于 HBM 与大宗DRAM 晶圆分配之间出现了显著的此消彼长。鉴于现有产能有限，且未来两年内新增晶圆产能到位尚需时日，这种产能博弈尤为明显。

paragraphs.source: The impact of materially expanding HBM capacity is unambiguous. When we examine HBM’s wafer intensity within overall DRAM production, we find that HBM wafer capacity as a share of total DRAM wafer capacity at Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron was below 5% in 2022, but has already increased to approximately 20% by year-end 2025. By the end of 2027, we estimate this figure will reach ~35%. This implies that HBM alone will consume more than one-third of the combined DRAM wafer capacity across the three suppliers, directly displacing capacity that would otherwise be available for commodity DRAM production.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The impact of materially expanding HBM capacity is unambiguous. When we examine HBM’s wafer intensity within overall DRAM production, we find that HBM wafer capacity as a share of total DRAM wafer capacity at Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron was below 5% in 2022, but has already increased to approximately 20% by year-end 2025. By the end of 2027, we estimate this figure will reach ~35%. This implies that HBM alone will consume more than one-third of the combined DRAM wafer capacity across the three suppliers, directly displacing capacity that would otherwise be available for commodity DRAM production.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: The impact of materially expanding HBM capacity is unambiguous. When we examine HBM’s wafer intensity within overall DRAM production, we find that HBM wafer capacity as a share of total DRAM wafer capacity at Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron was below 5% in 2022, but has already increased to approximately 20% by year-end 2025. By the end of 2027, we estimate this figure will reach ~35%. This implies that HBM alone will consume more than one-third of the combined DRAM wafer capacity across the three suppliers, directly displacing capacity that would otherwise be available for commodity DRAM production.

pro.text: 大幅扩张 HBM 产能的影响毋庸置疑。审视 HBM 在整体 DRAM 生产中的晶圆消耗比重，我们发现，2022 年三星、SK海力士与美光的 HBM 晶圆产能占 DRAM 总晶圆产能的比例尚不足 5%，但到 2025 年底已攀升至约 20%。我们预计，到 2027 年底该数字将达到约 35%。这意味着仅 HBM 一项就将吞噬三大供应商超过三分之一的 DRAM 晶圆总产能，直接挤占原本可用于生产大宗DRAM 的产能。

paragraphs.source: One may ask why memory makers are moving so aggressively to expand HBM capacity. We believe the rationale is twofold. First, HBM is increasingly viewed as a structural and sustainable growth engine that extends well beyond traditional memory end markets such as PCs, mobile, and automotive. This is reinforced by the continued increase in HBM intensity within AI servers (both GPU and ASIC), where HBM remains indispensable for both model training and inference. As a result, rising HBM content per AI server should meaningfully drive incremental demand going forward.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: One may ask why memory makers are moving so aggressively to expand HBM capacity. We believe the rationale is twofold. First, HBM is increasingly viewed as a structural and sustainable growth engine that extends well beyond traditional memory end markets such as PCs, mobile, and automotive. This is reinforced by the continued increase in HBM intensity within AI servers (both GPU and ASIC), where HBM remains indispensable for both model training and inference. As a result, rising HBM content per AI server should meaningfully drive incremental demand going forward.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: One may ask why memory makers are moving so aggressively to expand HBM capacity. We believe the rationale is twofold. First, HBM is increasingly viewed as a structural and sustainable growth engine that extends well beyond traditional memory end markets such as PCs, mobile, and automotive. This is reinforced by the continued increase in HBM intensity within AI servers (both GPU and ASIC), where HBM remains indispensable for both model training and inference. As a result, rising HBM content per AI server should meaningfully drive incremental demand going forward.

pro.text: 人们不禁要问，存储制造商为何如此激进地扩张 HBM 产能。我们认为背后有双重驱动因素。首先，HBM 愈发被视为一种结构性且可持续的增长引擎，其应用边界已远超 PC、移动设备和汽车等传统存储终端市场。AI 服务器（包括 GPU 和 ASIC）中 HBM 搭载量的持续攀升进一步印证了这一点，无论是在模型训练还是推理环节，HBM 都不可或缺。因此，单台 AI 服务器 HBM 容量的提升必将大幅拉动未来的增量需求。

paragraphs.source: Second, HBM’s complex front-end and back-end manufacturing requirements enable greater product differentiation relative to commodity DRAM. Performance characteristics such as pin speed, power efficiency, thermal capability, and packaging integration vary meaningfully by supplier compared to conventional DRAM, allowing memory makers to distinguish their products more clearly. This differentiation creates room for pricing power, incremental margin capture, and market share gain, in contrast to commodity DRAM where competition is driven largely by cost and scale. SK hynix’ success in both HBM3 and HBM3E should be the best example for this.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Second, HBM’s complex front-end and back-end manufacturing requirements enable greater product differentiation relative to commodity DRAM. Performance characteristics such as pin speed, power efficiency, thermal capability, and packaging integration vary meaningfully by supplier compared to conventional DRAM, allowing memory makers to distinguish their products more clearly. This differentiation creates room for pricing power, incremental margin capture, and market share gain, in contrast to commodity DRAM where competition is driven largely by cost and scale. SK hynix’ success in both HBM3 and HBM3E should be the best example for this.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Second, HBM’s complex front-end and back-end manufacturing requirements enable greater product differentiation relative to commodity DRAM. Performance characteristics such as pin speed, power efficiency, thermal capability, and packaging integration vary meaningfully by supplier compared to conventional DRAM, allowing memory makers to distinguish their products more clearly. This differentiation creates room for pricing power, incremental margin capture, and market share gain, in contrast to commodity DRAM where competition is driven largely by cost and scale. SK hynix’ success in both HBM3 and HBM3E should be the best example for this.

pro.text: 其次，相较于大宗DRAM，HBM 复杂的前道与后道制造要求赋予了产品更大的差异化空间。与传统 DRAM 相比，不同供应商在引脚速度、能效、散热能力以及封装集成度等性能特征上存在显著差异，这使得存储制造商能够更清晰地凸显自身产品优势。这种差异化为获取定价权、攫取增量利润率以及抢占市场份额创造了空间，这与主要依靠成本和规模驱动竞争的大宗DRAM 截然不同。SK海力士在 HBM3 和 HBM3E 上的成功便是最佳佐证。

Second-order impact: HBM production suffers a massive bit output penalty, yielding 3x to 4x fewer bits per wafer than commodity DRAM.
Node migration introduces execution friction, with new tool sets and process window re-optimization causing temporary yield and capacity declines.

paragraphs.source: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: Yet, such a significant expansion of HBM capacity carries meaningful cost and strategic challenges. As memory manufacturers increasingly allocate both existing and incremental wafer capacity to HBM, they not only materially constrain capacity available to traditional DRAM end markets but also reduce overall DRAM bit output due to HBM’s lower bit conversion rate. The result is an even tighter commodity DRAM supply environment, even as total industry wafer capacity expands—reinforcing supply pressure across the broader DRAM market.

pro.text: 然而，如此大规模的 HBM 产能扩张也带来了不容忽视的成本与战略挑战。随着存储制造商不断将现有及新增晶圆产能向 HBM 倾斜，他们不仅严重挤压了传统 DRAM 终端市场的可用产能，还因 HBM 较低的位元转换率拉低了整体 DRAM 的比特产出。其结果是，即便全行业的晶圆总产能不断扩张，大宗DRAM 的供应环境却愈发吃紧，进一步加剧了整个 DRAM 市场的供应压力。

paragraphs.source: The first-order impact requires little explanation: reallocating wafer capacity to HBM directly reduces the number of wafers available for commodity DRAM production. However, the second-order effect is equally important and sometimes underappreciated. A wafer dedicated to commodity DRAM typically delivers roughly 3× the bit output of a wafer dedicated to HBM in the case of HBM3E 12-Hi. Based on our estimates, this gap is likely to widen to nearly 4× as the industry transitions to HBM4 later this year and potentially even more in HBM4E the following year.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: The first-order impact requires little explanation: reallocating wafer capacity to HBM directly reduces the number of wafers available for commodity DRAM production. However, the second-order effect is equally important and sometimes underappreciated. A wafer dedicated to commodity DRAM typically delivers roughly 3× the bit output of a wafer dedicated to HBM in the case of HBM3E 12-Hi. However, the second-order effect is equally important and sometimes underappreciated. A wafer dedicated to commodity DRAM typically delivers roughly 3× the bit output of a wafer dedicated to HBM in the case of HBM3E 12-Hi. Based on our estimates, this gap is likely to widen to nearly 4× as the industry transitions to HBM4 later this year and potentially even more in HBM4E the following year.

pro.text: 一阶影响不言而喻：将晶圆产能重新分配给 HBM，会直接减少可用于生产标准型 DRAM 的晶圆数量。然而，二阶效应同样重要，却往往被低估。以 HBM3E 12-Hi（12 层堆叠）为例，专门用于生产标准型 DRAM 的晶圆，其比特产出通常约为 HBM 专属晶圆的 3 倍。根据我们的测算，随着今年晚些时候行业向 HBM4 过渡，这一差距可能扩大至近 4 倍；到明年推出 HBM4E 时，差距甚至会进一步拉大。

paragraphs.source: HBM uses larger die sizes as each HBM stack consists of multiple large DRAM dies optimized for bandwidth rather than density. HBM’s larger die size is due to the presence of through-silicon vias (TSVs), which require dedicated keep-out zones that reduce usable array area and inflate die size. This materially reduces the number of dies that can be harvested per wafer compared to commodity DRAM dies. HBM, compared to commodity DDR DRAM, also demands much greater cell performance, which dramatically reduces front-end sort yields for HBM wafers.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: HBM uses larger die sizes as each HBM stack consists of multiple large DRAM dies optimized for bandwidth rather than density. HBM’s larger die size is due to the presence of through-silicon vias (TSVs), which require dedicated keep-out zones that reduce usable array area and inflate die size. This materially reduces the number of dies that can be harvested per wafer compared to commodity DRAM dies. HBM, compared to commodity DDR DRAM, also demands much greater cell performance, which dramatically reduces front-end sort yields for HBM wafers.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: HBM uses larger die sizes as each HBM stack consists of multiple large DRAM dies optimized for bandwidth rather than density. HBM’s larger die size is due to the presence of through-silicon vias (TSVs), which require dedicated keep-out zones that reduce usable array area and inflate die size. This materially reduces the number of dies that can be harvested per wafer compared to commodity DRAM dies. HBM, compared to commodity DDR DRAM, also demands much greater cell performance, which dramatically reduces front-end sort yields for HBM wafers.

pro.text: HBM 采用更大的裸片尺寸，因为每个 HBM 堆叠均由多个大尺寸 DRAM 裸片组成，这些裸片专为带宽而非存储密度进行了优化。HBM 裸片尺寸较大，归因于硅通孔（TSV）的存在。TSV 需要专属的禁布区，这不仅缩减了可用的阵列面积，还导致裸片尺寸膨胀。与大宗DRAM 裸片相比，这大幅减少了单片晶圆上可切割出的合格裸片数量。此外，相较于大宗 DDR DRAM，HBM 对存储单元的性能要求苛刻得多，这导致 HBM 晶圆的前道分选良率一路陡降。

paragraphs.source: HBM also requires TSV and a series of incremental process steps—including wafer thinning and backside processing—that do not exist in standard DRAM manufacturing. These steps add complexity and introduce incremental yield loss, further reducing effective bit output per wafer.

These steps add complexity and introduce incremental yield loss, further reducing effective bit output per wafer.

pro.text: HBM 还需要硅通孔以及一系列新增工艺步骤——包括晶圆减薄与背面加工——这些在标准型 DRAM 制造中并不存在。这些步骤不仅增加了工艺复杂性，还带来了额外的良率折损，进一步拉低了单片晶圆的有效比特产出。

paragraphs.source: Stacking and advanced packaging (primary bonding) yields also meaningfully impact HBM output. Even when individual DRAM die yields are high, the compounded yield across 8-high or 12-high stacks is structurally lower, as a single defective die can compromise the usability of an entire stack. This stands in contrast to commodity DRAM, where each die is sold independently, and yield losses are more localized. Finally, HBM products prioritize bandwidth and power efficiency over maximum density, limiting the degree to which bit scaling can offset these inherent inefficiencies.

pro.text: 堆叠与先进封装（主要指键合）的良率同样对 HBM 产出产生重大影响。即使单个 DRAM 裸片良率很高，8层或12层堆叠的复合良率在结构上依然偏低，因为哪怕只有一个裸片存在缺陷，也会导致整个堆叠失效。这与标准型 DRAM 形成鲜明对比：后者的每个裸片均独立销售，良率折损仅限于单个裸片。最后，HBM 产品将带宽与能效置于最高存储密度之上，因此，通过比特缩放来弥补这些固有低效的空间十分有限。

paragraphs.source: We think what distinguishes this cycle is that the product transition driving demand - HBM - is far more manufacturing-intensive, effectively introducing a form of reverse scaling. Unlike prior computing platform inflections that primarily expanded demand without materially constraining supply, the AI-driven shift toward HBM simultaneously boosts demand while tightening supply. This dynamic is further amplified by rising server DRAM content, including emerging LPDDR5-based products such as SOCAMM and SOCAMM2.
paragraphs.parent_block_markdown: We think what distinguishes this cycle is that the product transition driving demand - HBM - is far more manufacturing-intensive, effectively introducing a form of reverse scaling. Unlike prior computing platform inflections that primarily expanded demand without materially constraining supply, the AI-driven shift toward HBM simultaneously boosts demand while tightening supply. This dynamic is further amplified by rising server DRAM content, including emerging LPDDR5-based products such as SOCAMM and SOCAMM2.
paragraphs.parent_block_plain_text: We think what distinguishes this cycle is that the product transition driving demand - HBM - is far more manufacturing-intensive, effectively introducing a form of reverse scaling. Unlike prior computing platform inflections that primarily expanded demand without materially constraining supply, the AI-driven shift toward HBM simultaneously boosts demand while tightening supply. This dynamic is further amplified by rising server DRAM content, including emerging LPDDR5-based products such as SOCAMM and SOCAMM2.

pro.text: 我们认为，本轮周期的独特之处在于，驱动需求的产品迭代——即 HBM——对制造资源的消耗远超以往，实际上引入了一种反向缩放。此前的计算平台变革主要在于扩大需求，并未对供给造成实质性约束；而此次由 AI 驱动的 HBM 转型，在推升需求的同时收紧了供给。随着服务器 DRAM 单机容量的攀升，包括 SOCAMM 与 SOCAMM2 等基于 LPDDR5 的新兴产品不断涌现，这一供需动态被进一步放大。

applied_terms: Maintain a strict distinction between 'wafer capacity' (晶圆产能) and 'bit output' (比特产出).
Manufacturing terms must sound native: 'execution friction' (执行摩擦/落地阻力), 'process windows' (工艺窗口), 'yields deteriorate' (良率下降/爬坡期良率折损).

key_points: First-order impact: Reallocating wafers to HBM directly starves commodity DRAM production.
Second-order impact: HBM production suffers a massive bit output penalty, yielding 3x to 4x fewer bits per wafer than commodity DRAM.
Node migration introduces execution friction, with new tool sets and process window re-optimization causing temporary yield and capacity declines.

issues.description: 译文在绝大多数段落（如段落 11、18、23、24 等）将 Commodity DRAM 译为“大宗DRAM”，这直接违反了 Translation Guidelines 中明确要求的“Translate 'Commodity DRAM' as '标准型 DRAM'”，且与译文自身在段落 32、35 中使用的“标准型 DRAM”前后不一。此外，“大宗”与“DRAM”之间缺失了标准的中西文空格。

将 ASIC 直接完全汉化为“专用集成电路”，而同级别的 GPU 却保留了英文缩写。

paragraphs.source: NVIDIA, for example, the transition from Blackwell to Blackwell Ultra and Rubin increases HBM capacity by ~50%, with Rubin Ultra driving a further ~4× jump in system memory from 288 GB to ~1 TB. Similarly, ASICs such as Goole’s TPU v8AX and Amazon’s Trainium3 also migrating from HBM3E 8-Hi to 12-Hi stacks, while AMD’s memory capacity increases materially from ~288 GB in MI350 to ~432 GB in MI400. At a high level, it is not difficult to envision the magnitude of DRAM demand required to support both existing and next-generation AI compute platforms.

Similarly, ASICs such as Goole’s TPU v8AX and Amazon’s Trainium3 also migrating from HBM3E 8-Hi to 12-Hi stacks, while AMD’s memory capacity increases materially from ~288 GB in MI350 to ~432 GB in MI400. Similarly, ASICs such as Goole’s TPU v8AX and Amazon’s Trainium3 also migrating from HBM3E 8-Hi to 12-Hi stacks, while AMD’s memory capacity increases materially from ~288 GB in MI350 to ~432 GB in MI400. pro.text: 以英伟达（NVIDIA）为例，从 Blackwell 向 Blackwell Ultra 及 Rubin 的过渡，使 HBM 容量提升了约 50%；而 Rubin Ultra 更是推动系统内存实现约 4 倍的跨越式增长，从 288 GB 跃升至约 1 TB。同样，谷歌（Google）的张量处理器（TPU）v8AX 与亚马逊（Amazon）的 Trainium3 等 ASIC 芯片，也正从 HBM3E 8层堆叠向 12层堆叠迁移；同时，AMD 的内存容量也实现了大幅增长，从 MI350 的约 288 GB 增至 MI400 的约 432 GB。从宏观层面来看，要支撑现有及下一代 AI 计算平台，所需的 DRAM 需求规模之大已不言而喻。

paragraphs.source: We estimate that HBM demand alone will account for more than 10% of total DRAM demand by the end of 2027, layered on top of the already substantial server DRAM contribution, which is about 40% of total DRAM end market. Given the DRAM content increases discussed earlier and robust demand across both AI and general-purpose servers, we believe the importance of servers within the overall DRAM end market will continue to increase over the next few years, and is already seen as a top priority for memory suppliers over other categories.

Given the DRAM content increases discussed earlier and robust demand across both AI and general-purpose servers, we believe the importance of servers within the overall DRAM end market will continue to increase over the next few years, and is already seen as a top priority for memory suppliers over other categories.

pro.text: 我们预计，到 2027 年底，单是 HBM 需求就将占据 DRAM 总需求的 10% 以上。这还需叠加本就十分可观的服务器 DRAM 份额（约占 DRAM 终端市场总量的 40%）。鉴于前文探讨的 DRAM 单机容量增长，加上 AI 与通用服务器双双展现强劲需求，我们认为未来几年内，服务器在整体 DRAM 终端市场中的重要性必将持续攀升。存储供应商也早已将其视为力压其他品类的首要战略重心。
